data.title: Bombardier to partially redeem $300 million of senior notes
data.description:
• Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) said it has issued a notice of partial redemption for $300 million aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 7.875% Senior Notes due 2027.
  • The redemption will be paid using cash from its balance sheet.
  • The firm said the redemption date is January 9, 2025, and the redemption price for the 2027 Notes is 100.00% of the principal amount redeemed, plus accrued and unpaid interest.

data.title: Oracle skids, but Wall Street comes to defense as cloud growth continues
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) may have reported fiscal second-quarter results and guidance that were a bit weaker-than-expected, but Wall Street continued to praise the IT giant nonetheless, amid continued growth in the cloud.

Shares were down 6% in premarket trading, while other cloud software stocks were also lower, including Microsoft (MSFT), Adobe (ADBE) and ServiceNow (NOW).

"A fourth consecutive quarter of accelerating [current remaining performance obligations] growth to 20% y/y (vs. 18% last quarter) reinforces our confidence in a multi-quarter growth reacceleration that could evolve into a multi-year acceleration, underpinned by cloud and AI secular tailwinds, particularly as Meta joins companies like xAI, NVIDIA, and Cohere as customers leveraging Oracle's AI infrastructure," Piper Sandler analyst Brent Bracelin wrote in a note to clients.

"While the Oracle Cloud momentum was encouraging, consistent expense discipline coupled with improving margins across cloud applications and infrastructure was equally encouraging," Bracelin added. He raised his price target to $210 from $185 after the results and reiterated his Overweight rating.

Mizuho Securities analyst Siti Panagrahi also raised his price target (to $210 from $185) on Oracle, as he pointed out the cloud strength is being driven by artificial intelligence.

"Oracle's broad portfolio of infrastructure and application products, its multi-cloud strategy through hyperscaler partnerships, and unprecedented AI demand position it well to reaccelerate growth," Panagrahi wrote in a note to clients. "We view the current share weakness as a buying opportunity."

KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst Jackson Ader noted that while the dip in the stock was the result of some "lofty expectations," bookings momentum is still on track and acceleration in cloud and software-as-a-service businesses are still expected to expand. "We still like Oracle heading into 2025," Ader wrote. He has an Overweight rating and $200 price target on the stock.

Looking to the next quarter, Oracle said it expects to earn between $1.50 and $1.54 per share on an adjusted basis (below the $1.57 per share estimate), with revenue growing between 7% and 9% year-over-year, or 9% to 11% in constant currency. It also remains very confident to see double-digit growth in full-year revenue, while cloud infrastructure should see 50% or more growth.

data.title: AllianceBernstein launches two active buffer ETFs
data.description:
• Investment management firm AllianceBernstein Holding (NYSE:AB) has launched two active buffer ETFs - AB International Buffer ETF and AB Moderate Buffer ETF on Nasdaq.
  • "Today's launch is a direct result of the ongoing interest in our first buffer ETF, AB Conservative Buffer ETF (BUFC)," AllianceBernstein's head of ETFs and portfolio solutions, Noel Archard.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Saia ups borrowing capacity to $600M
data.description:
• Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) said it has amended its unsecured revolving credit facility to increase its borrowing capacity from $300 million to $600 million.
  • The amendment also extends the maturity of the existing credit facility to December 2029.
  • The firm said the amendment also ups the availability under an accordion feature from $150 million to $300 million.

data.title: AutoZone in charts: Total store count shows steady Y/Y growth, while comp sales decelerate
Ollie's Bargain Outlet Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.01, revenue of $517.4M misses by $2.07M
data.description:
• Ollie's Bargain Outlet press release (NASDAQ:OLLI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $517.4M (+7.8% Y/Y) misses by $2.07M.
  • Net sales increased 7.8% to $517.4 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2024 as compared with net sales of $480.1 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2023. The increase in net sales was the result of new store unit growth, partially offset by a comparable store sales decrease of 0.5%.
  • Comparable store sales decreased 0.5% from the prior year increase of 7.0%.
  • The Company opened 24 new stores and closed three stores, including two permanent closures and one temporary closure related to Hurricane Helene, ending the quarter with 546 stores in 31 states, a year-over-year increase in store count of 8.1%.
  • New Previous
    New store openings 50 50
    Store closures 3 2
    Net sales $2.270 to $2.280 billion $2.276 to $2.291 billion
    Comparable store sales increase 2.7% to 3.0% 2.7% to 3.2%
    Gross margin 40.0% 40.0%
    Operating income $251 to $258 million $252 to $259 million
    Adjusted net income(2) $199 to $203 million $199 to $203 million
    Adjusted net income per diluted share(2) $3.22 to $3.30 $3.22 to $3.30
    Annual effective tax rate (excludes excess tax benefits related to stock-based compensation) 25.0% 25.0%
    Diluted weighted average shares outstanding 62 million 62 million
    Capital expenditures $104 million $104 million

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange launches long EU bond index futures
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) introduced Long European Union Bond Index futures on Tuesday along with the first trades in the contract, it said in a statement.

The cash-settled futures contract references the ICE 8-13 Year European Union Index (Ticker: G0EU8T13), which tracks the performance of long-term debt issued by the EU.

"With the interest and debate around the status of European Union bonds not being included in sovereign bond indices, we saw that customers wanted a way to invest in, and manage, exposure to EU debt," said Varun Pawar, chief product officer of ICE Fixed Income and Data Services.

The product "provides a much-needed solution for managing exposure to EU sovereign bonds efficiently," said Mirco Bulega, partner at ExodusPoint Capital Management U.K.

ICE Data Indices, recognized as a third country benchmark administrator under the U.K. Benchmarks Regulation, produced the index, which is a subset of the ICE European Union Index, including all securities with a remaining term to final maturity greater than or equal to eight years and less than 13 years. The index tracks exposure to a specific point on the EU curve, and the historical volatility of the index is similar to other European bond futures.

data.title: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital prices offering of $300M notes
data.description:
• HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital (NYSE:HASI) has priced its private offering of $300M of 6.375% green senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be additional notes and form part of the same class as the company’s existing 6.375% green senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • Net proceeds from the offering will be approximately $297M that will be used to temporarily repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under the company’s unsecured credit facility and its commercial paper program.
  • The company will use cash equal to the net proceeds from the offering to acquire, invest in or refinance, in whole or in part, new and/or existing eligible green projects.

data.title: AutoZone misses estimates with its FQ1 report, but points to improving trends
data.description:
AutoZone (NYSE:AZO) is on watch after falling short of estimates with its Q3 earnings report. Domestic same-store sales were up 0.3% during the quarter vs. +0.7% consensus, and international same-store sales were up 1.0% vs 1.02% consensus. Total revenue was up 1.9% year-over-year to $4.28 billion, but missed the consensus estimate by $30 million.

Operating profit fell 0.9% during the quarter to $841.1 million. Net income for the quarter was $564.9 million compared to $593.5 million in the same period last year, while diluted earnings per share were $32.52 vs. $33.64 consensus and the $32.55 mark from a year ago.

Under its share repurchase program, AutoZone (AZO) repurchased 160K shares of its common stock during the first quarter, at an average price per share of $3,156, for a total investment of $505.2 million.

During the quarter ended November 23, 2024, AutoZone (AZO) opened 23 new stores in the U.S., six in Mexico and five in Brazil for a total of 34 new stores. As of November 23, the company had 6,455 stores in the U.S., 800 in Mexico and 132 in Brazil for a total store count of 7,387.

CEO Phil Daniele pointed to progress in DIY same store sales result from the prior quarter as average ticket and traffic trends improved. "Our domestic Commercial sales were up 3.2% and we were encouraged by the improving trends seen at the end of the quarter," he noted. Daniele said currency rate moves depressed reported international sales and earnings growth.

Looking ahead, AutoZone (AZO) said it is well positioned for growth heading into the remainder of the fiscal year. "As we continue to invest in our business, we remain committed to our disciplined approach of a focus on increasing earnings and operating cash flow, all while delivering strong shareholder value," highlighted Daniele.

Shares of AutoZone (AZO) tricked 0.1% higher in premarket action on Tuesday. The auto retail stock is up almost 29% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: AutoZone FQ1 misses top-line and bottom-line estimates
data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg
